carbon_essentials.overview: Core Carbon Design System knowledge needed for any Carbon-related task, including component discovery, Carbon MCP usage, and fundamental design tokens.

carbon_mcp_usage.title: Using Carbon MCP for Documentation and Code Examples
carbon_mcp_usage.description: The Carbon MCP server provides two essential tools for working with Carbon: docs_search for documentation and code_search for implementation examples.

tool.name: docs_search
tool.purpose: Search Carbon and IBM Products documentation for design, usage, style, accessibility, and general content

when_to_use.use: Understanding component guidelines and best practices
Learning about design patterns and usage

pattern.name: AI Chat Documentation
pattern.description: Search AI Chat documentation (DO NOT use component filters)
pattern.critical_rule: For AI Chat documentation, query by API, symbol, or topic name. DO NOT use component_id or component_type filters.

pattern.name: General Topics
pattern.description: Search for design patterns, accessibility, or general guidance

tool.name: code_search
tool.purpose: Search Carbon React/Web Components code examples, icons, pictograms, and AI Chat example code

when_to_use.use: Finding implementation examples with correct props

pattern.name: React Component Examples
pattern.description: Search for React component implementation examples

pattern.name: AI Chat Examples
pattern.description: Search AI Chat code examples (include framework in query)
pattern.critical_rule: For AI Chat examples, include "ai chat" plus the framework name in the query. DO NOT use component_type filter.

pattern.name: Icons and Pictograms
pattern.description: Search for icons or pictograms (DO NOT use component_type filter)

research_workflow.title: MANDATORY: Research Before Implementation
research_workflow.rule: ALWAYS use Carbon MCP to research correct component syntax and API BEFORE writing or updating ANY Carbon component code

enforcement.requirement: For EVERY Carbon component you plan to use, you MUST call code_search FIRST
Create a component research checklist using update_todo_list for implementations with 3+ components
Document the props and patterns found from code_search before writing any code
If you write Carbon component code without prior code_search, this is a CRITICAL ERROR

steps.step: Identify ALL Carbon components needed for the implementation
Use code_search to find implementation examples for EACH component
Use docs_search to understand guidelines and best practices
Review examples for correct props and usage patterns
Document findings (props, variants, patterns) before implementing

carbon_packages.title: Carbon React Libraries and Packages
carbon_packages.description: Carbon React libraries are defined as Carbon v11 packages. Always prefer using official packages over recreating components.

mcp_integration.instruction: Use the Carbon MCP to discover and retrieve component information dynamically. Do NOT rely on static lists - always query the MCP for current, detailed information.

step.example: code_search with filters: {"component_type": "React", "component_id": "button"} Returns: variants, props, imports, examples, Storybook links
code_search with filters: {"ibm_products": "yes", "component_id": "page-header"} Returns: IBM Products components with full variant details

step.critical_rule: NEVER use code_search for charts. Always use get_charts tool. First call with mode:"schema" to see variants and data/options structure. Then call with mode:"full" and chosen variant for complete source code.
step.example: get_charts with framework:"react", chart_type:"line", mode:"schema" Then: get_charts with framework:"react", chart_type:"line", variant:"index", mode:"full"
code_search with filters: {"asset_type": "icon"} code_search with filters: {"asset_type": "pictogram"}

step.action: For usage guidelines, accessibility, or style specifications
step.example: docs_search with query:"Button usage" and filters: {"component_id": "button"} Returns: Usage guidelines, accessibility info, style specifications

benefits.benefit: Always current with latest Carbon releases
More detailed than static lists (variants, props, examples)

package.name: @carbon/react
package.discovery: Use code_search with component_type:"React" to discover available components

preview_components.description: Preview components are experimental features that may have breaking changes. They require special import syntax using the preview__ prefix.

identification.how_to_identify: When MCP code_search returns a component, check for preview indicators: - Import statements contain "preview__" prefix - Storybook title includes "Preview/" path - Component rag_id or name contains "preview"

preview_components.discovery: Preview components are automatically discoverable via code_search. MCP results will include the correct import syntax with preview__ prefix. No static list is needed - rely on MCP to identify current preview components.

package.name: @carbon/ibm-products
package.description: IBM-specific product components (Carbon for IBM Products)
package.discovery: Use code_search with ibm_products:"yes" to discover available components

package.name: @carbon/web-components

special_requirements.requirement: Browser-only code - requires SSR handling if project uses SSR
Must use React.lazy() for dynamic import in SSR projects
See working_practices.xml for SSR awareness guidance

package.import_pattern: // For SSR projects, use dynamic import import '@carbon/web-components/es/components/chat/index.js';

package.name: @carbon/icons-react
package.usage: Use for all icons in the application

package.name: @carbon/charts-react
package.description: Carbon chart components for data visualization
package.discovery: Use get_charts tool (NOT code_search) with framework:"react" and chart_type. Available chart types: line, area, bar, pie, donut, gauge, scatter, bubble, combo, radar, treemap, heatmap, meter
package.critical_rule: NEVER use code_search for Carbon Charts. Always use get_charts tool. Call with mode:"schema" first to see variants, then mode:"full" for complete source.

rule.tag: Classifies content — "what is this?" (category, label, keyword). Variants: read-only, dismissible, selectable, operational.
rule.critical_rule: NEVER use colored Tag for status — use IconIndicator/ShapeIndicator instead.
rule.decision: Classification (what it IS) → Tag | State (what's HAPPENING) → IconIndicator/ShapeIndicator
rule.note: Both can coexist: Tag = what it is, Status Indicator = what it's doing.

rule.title: Tabs Component Pairing
rule.description: Tabs components must be used in specific pairs depending on orientation. Horizontal and vertical tabs use different component sets.

horizontal_tabs.usage: Use for horizontal tab navigation (default orientation)
horizontal_tabs.critical_rule: ALWAYS use Tabs with TabList for horizontal tabs
horizontal_tabs.example:
import { Tabs, TabList, Tab, TabPanels, TabPanel } from '@carbon/react';
<Tabs> <TabList aria-label="List of tabs"> <Tab>Tab 1</Tab> <Tab>Tab 2</Tab> <Tab>Tab 3</Tab> </TabList> <TabPanels> <TabPanel>Content 1</TabPanel> <TabPanel>Content 2</TabPanel> <TabPanel>Content 3</TabPanel> </TabPanels> </Tabs>

vertical_tabs.critical_rule: ALWAYS use TabsVertical with TabListVertical for vertical tabs
vertical_tabs.example:
import { TabsVertical, TabListVertical, Tab, TabPanels, TabPanel } from '@carbon/react';
<TabsVertical> <TabListVertical aria-label="List of vertical tabs"> <Tab>Tab 1</Tab> <Tab>Tab 2</Tab> <Tab>Tab 3</Tab> </TabListVertical> <TabPanels> <TabPanel>Content 1</TabPanel> <TabPanel>Content 2</TabPanel> <TabPanel>Content 3</TabPanel> </TabPanels> </TabsVertical>

critical_rules.rule: Tab, TabPanels, and TabPanel components work with both orientations

rule.decision: Horizontal tabs → Tabs + TabList | Vertical tabs → TabsVertical + TabListVertical

package_selection_guidelines.guideline: Always use official Carbon packages rather than recreating components
Add required dependencies when needed (recommend to user)
Check if component exists in @carbon/react first, then @carbon/ibm-products
Use Carbon MCP to verify component availability and correct package

design_tokens.title: Carbon Design Tokens
design_tokens.description: Carbon provides design tokens for consistent styling. Always use tokens instead of hardcoded values to ensure theme compatibility.

theme_tokens.title: Theme Tokens (Semantic)
theme_tokens.description: Semantic tokens that adapt to the current theme. Always prefer theme tokens over color tokens for theme adaptability.

spacing_tokens.title: Spacing Tokens
spacing_tokens.description: Tokens for consistent spacing throughout the UI. Use Carbon MCP to look up current token values. Spacing follows 8px increments.

typography_tokens.title: Typography Tokens
typography_tokens.description: Type tokens for consistent typography. Use type mixins instead of manual font declarations.

layer_system.title: Layer System for Theme Context
layer_system.description: The Layer component provides theme context for nested components. Use it when you need background color changes that match layer tokens.

critical_guidelines.guideline: NEVER manually set the layer level using the level prop. Let nesting determine layer level automatically.
Use withBackground prop when the design shows a visible background color. Without withBackground, Layer only provides context without setting background.

component_discovery.title: Component Discovery Process
component_discovery.description: Process for finding the right Carbon component for a given use case.

step.action: Identify the UI pattern or functionality needed
step.example: Need a data table with sorting and pagination

step.action: Use Carbon MCP code_search to find similar examples
Use Carbon MCP docs_search to understand component guidelines
Review examples and documentation to understand props and patterns
Implement using verified patterns from Carbon MCP

project_specific_patterns.title: Project-Specific Component Recognition
project_specific_patterns.description: When analyzing designs, check for existing project-specific implementations of common patterns like global headers, navigation, or footers.

global_header_recognition.title: Global Header Recognition
global_header_recognition.description: Many projects have a reusable global header component. When analyzing designs with global headers, check if the project already has an implementation before creating a new one.

guidance.point: Use search_files to find header-related components
Look for files like CommonHeader, GlobalHeader, AppHeader
Check in common locations: src/components/, src/layouts/

step.action: Read the component source if found

guidance.point: Use read_file to examine the existing implementation
Document the component's location and usage
Note any props or customization options

step.action: Note in assessment and focus on page-specific content

guidance.point: Acknowledge the existing global header in your assessment
Focus analysis on page-specific content below the header
Don't duplicate or recreate the global header

step.example_note: ## Global Header This project uses CommonHeader at `src/components/commonHeader/CommonHeader.jsx` for the global header. The analysis below focuses on page-specific content.

benefits.benefit: Focuses implementation effort on new features

pattern.name: Navigation
pattern.description: Check for existing Nav or Sidebar components

integration_principle.title: Respect Existing Patterns
integration_principle.description: Always check for and use existing project-specific components before creating new ones. This maintains consistency and reduces duplication.

pattern.name: Basic Layout
pattern.code:
import { Grid, Column } from '@carbon/react';

Page Title

pattern.name: Form with Validation
pattern.code:
import { TextInput, Button, Form } from '@carbon/react';
<Form> <TextInput id="email" labelText="Email" invalid={hasError} invalidText="Please enter a valid email" /> <Button type="submit">Submit</Button> </Form>

pattern.name: Modal Dialog
